title: Arisaema parisifolium
intro: Arisaema parisifolium, also known as the Paris Lily, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Arum family (Arisaemaceae). Traditionally, Arisaema parisifolium has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Arisaema parisifolium is often prepared as a dried root or stem, and is commonly used to make teas, decoctions, and infusions.
taxonomy: Arisaema parisifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Arum, commonly known as the Paris-lily or Arum lily. The genus Arisaema is a group of perennial plants found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Arisaema parisifolium is native to the mountainous regions of southern China. It is known for its unique, solitary leaf and the way it wraps around the stem. The classification of Arisaema parisifolium places it within the family Araceae, subfamily Aroideae.
